description_long: This is a two-story suburban house surrounded by a semicircle of various trees, including both green and purple-leaved varieties. The house features a prominent front porch with columns, a gabled roof, and a single-car garage attached to the right side. The exterior is finished in light-colored siding with darker roofing shingles, and the windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern on the front facade. The rear of the house has a smaller porch or deck area, and the side elevations are simpler with fewer windows. The landscaping is minimal, with the trees providing a natural boundary around the property. The overall design suggests a comfortable, family-oriented residence typical of North American neighborhoods.
description: A two-story suburban house with a gabled roof, front porch, attached garage, and surrounded by a semicircle of green and purple trees.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, columns, attached garage, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the plain siding, minimal windows, and the surrounding trees.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a smaller porch or deck and a more modest window arrangement.
description_view_3: Right side view with simple siding, a few windows, and the tree boundary.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry features a covered porch with columns, steps, and a single front door, flanked by windows and sheltered by an overhang.
